name: Self-Frosted Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 12 tablespoons Unsalted butter - cut into pieces and softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees. Grease an 8-inch springform pan. Line the base of the pan with parchment paper or foil; grease the paper or foil. Flour the side of the pan and lined base, tapping to remove excess.
2. Melt chocolate in a double boiler or large heatproof bowl over hot (not simmering) water at low heat, stirring occasionally. Stir until smooth. Add butter; stir until blended. Remove from pan of water and cool to body temperature.
3. Sift flour and baking powder into a small bowl.
4. Beat egg yolks lightly in a large bowl. Beat in 12 tablespoons sugar; continue beating at high speed for about 5 minutes or until the mixture is pale and very thick. Stir in the melted chocolate mixture and vanilla.
5. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with medium-speed dry beaters until soft peaks form. Gradually beat in remaining sugar; continue beating at high speed until the whites are stiff and shiny but not dry.
6. Gently fold about 1/3 of the whites into the chocolate mixture until nearly incorporated. Fold in the remaining whites in two batches. Continue folding lightly but quickly, just until the mixture is blended.
7. Gently spoon 2 cups of the mixture into a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate. This will be used for frosting.
8. Sprinkle flour mixture over the remaining chocolate mixture; fold gently until the batter is blended.
9. Transfer the batter to the prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Cool in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes. Run a thin-bladed flexible knife around the side of the cake. Gently release the spring and remove the side of the pan. Let the cake cool until it is lukewarm; the cake will settle slightly in the center. Invert the cake onto another rack; remove the base of the pan. Carefully peel off the paper or foil; let the cake cool completely.
11. Allow the frosting mixture to stand at room temperature for about 1 hour, or until it is easy to spread.
12. Turn the cake onto a rack, then onto a platter so that the smooth side of the cake is facing up. Using a long metal spatula, spread the frosting evenly on the sides and top of the cake; swirl the top. Refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving. The cake can be kept, covered with a cake cover or large bowl, in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. Serve at room temperature.
